evals.prompt: Write a blameless postmortem. On Tuesday, a bad config change dropped the database tables. We restored from a backup, but there was 2 hours of downtime. The engineer didn't know the script would run in prod.
expectations: Uses blameless language (focuses on the lack of environment safeguards, not the engineer's mistake)
Includes a timeline of events (Detection, Mitigation, Resolution)
Includes a '5 Whys' root cause analysis
Includes actionable remediation steps (e.g., separating prod/dev credentials, adding confirmation prompts)

evals.prompt: Our payment API went down because the SSL certificate expired. Alerts fired but went to an email nobody checks. We realized when customers complained on Twitter.
evals.expected_output: Postmortem addressing monitoring and alerting failures.
expectations: Identifies the root cause (expired cert) and the secondary failure (alert routing)
Calculates Time to Detect (TTD) as unacceptably high due to relying on customer reports
Action items include fixing the cert renewal process AND fixing the PagerDuty/alert routing
